summary: AI is a multi-decade investment cycle analogous to the early internet, with ChatGPT representing only year three of a 20-30 year trend
summary_extended: Baker argues that AI represents a generational, multi-decade investment cycle analogous to the early internet, explicitly comparing ChatGPT's current moment to Netscape Navigator's role in the mid-1990s — suggesting we are only in year three of a 20-30 year trend. His reasoning draws on historical precedent: just as the internet took decades to fully reshape the economy, AI will similarly compound in importance and economic impact over an extended horizon. The investment implication is that the AI stack — infrastructure, software, and applications — represents a sustained, long-duration opportunity rather than a near-term trade, and Baker frames this view as foundational to his firm-building strategy at Atreides. He does not cite specific companies in this thesis, treating it as a macro-structural framing rather than a stock-specific call. Investors should resist the temptation to time or trade around AI and instead maintain long-duration exposure across the AI ecosystem.
detail: Baker explicitly compares ChatGPT to Netscape Navigator, suggesting we are in the very early innings of a generational technology cycle. He frames this as a 20-25-30 year trend, implying sustained investment opportunity across the AI stack for decades.
confidence_evidence: Baker states this as a personal belief framing his next chapter and firm-building strategy, not as a passing observation.
quote_fragment: ChatGPT is to AI as Netscape Navigator was
